title: Bajrang Baan
intro: Read Bajrang Baan with Hindi meaning. Recite this sacred prayer to seek the blessings of Lord Hanuman.
description:
Doha
Nishchay Prem Pratiti Te, Vinay Karain Sanmaan |
Tehi Ke Kaaraj Sakal Shubh, Siddh Karain Hanuman ||

Jay Hanumant Sant Hitkaari | Sun Leejai Prabhu Araj Hamaari ||
Jan Ke Kaaj Bilamb Na Keejai | Aatur Dauri Mahasukh Deejai ||
Jaise Koodi Sindhu Mahi Paara | Sursa Badan Paithi Vistaara ||
Aage Jaay Lankini Roka | Maarehu Laat Gayi Sur Loka ||
Jaay Vibhishan Ko Sukh Deenha | Sita Nirakhi Param-Pad Leena ||
Baag Ujaari Sindhu Mah Bora | Ati Aatur Jamkaatar Tora ||

Akshay Kumar Maari Sanhaara | Loom Lapeti Lank Ko Jaara ||
Laah Samaan Lank Jari Gayi | Jay-Jay Dhuni Surpur Mein Bhai ||
Ab Bilamb Kehi Kaaran Swami | Kripa Karahu Ur Antaryaami ||
Jay Jay Lakhan Praan Ke Daata | Aatur Hoi Duhkh Karahu Nipaata ||
Jai Giridhar Jai Jai Sukh Saagar | Sur-Samuh-Samrath Bhat-Naagar ||
Om Hanu Hanu Hanu Hanumant Hatheele | Bairihi Maaru Bajra Ki Keele ||

Gada Bajra Lai Bairihi Maaro | Mahaaraaj Prabhu Daas Ubaaro ||
Onkar Hunkaar Mahaprabhu Dhaao | Bajra Gada Hanu Bilamb Na Laao ||
Om Hneem Hneem Hneem Hanumant Kapeesa | Om Hum Hum Hum Hanu Ari Ur-Seesa ||
Satya Hohu Hari Shapath Paayke | Raam Doot Dharu Maaroo Jaayke ||

Jay Jay Jay Hanumant Agaadha | Duhkh Paavat Jan Kehi Apraadha ||
Puja Jap-Tap Nem Achaara | Nahin Jaanat Ho Daas Tumhaara ||
Van Upvan Mag Giri Grih Maanhin | Tumhare Bal Hum Darpat Naahin ||
Paayan Paraun Kar Jori Manaavon | Yehi Avasar Ab Kehi Goharavon ||
Jay Anjani Kumar Balvanta | Shankar Suvan Veer Hanumanta ||
Badan Karaal Kaal Kulghaalak | Raam Sahaay Sada Pratipaalak ||
Bhoot Pret Pisaach Nisaachar | Agin Vaitaal Kaal Maari Mar ||
Inhein Maaru, Tohi Shapath Raam Ki | Raakhau Naath Marjaad Naam Ki ||
Janaksuta Hari Daas Kahaavo | Taaki Shapath Bilamb Na Laavo ||
Jai Jai Jai Dhuni Hot Akaasa | Sumirat Hot Dusah Duhkh Naasa ||
Charan Sharan Kar Jori Manaavon | Yehi Avasar Ab Kehi Goharavon ||
Uthu Uthu Chalu Tohi Raam-Dohai | Paayan Paraun, Kar Jori Manaai ||
Om Cham Cham Cham Cham Chapal Chalanta | Om Hanu Hanu Hanu Hanu Hanumanta ||
Om Ham Ham Haank Det Kapi Chanchal | Om Sam Sam Sahami Paraane Khal-Dal ||
Apne Jan Ko Turat Ubaarau | Sumirat Hoy Aanand Hamaarau ||
Yeh Bajrang Baan Jehi Maarai | Taahi Kaho Phir Kon Ubaarai ||
Paath Karai Bajrang Baan Ki | Hanumat Raksha Karain Praan Ki ||
Yeh Bajrang Baan Jo Jaapain | Taate Bhoot-Pret Sab Kaapain ||
Dhoop Dey Aru Japai Hamesha | Taake Tan Nahin Rahai Kalesa ||

Doha
Prem Pratitihi Kapi Bhajai, Sada Dharai Ur Dhyaan |
Tehi Ke Kaaraj Sakal Shubh, Siddh Karain Hanuman ||

faq.q1: When should one recite the Bajrang Baan?
faq.a1: The Bajrang Baan is considered an "intense" (ugra) and highly powerful prayer. It is generally advised to recite it during times of severe crisis, when facing insurmountable obstacles, or when dealing with hidden enemies or negative forces. It is not typically recommended for trivial daily matters. Tuesdays and Saturdays are considered the most auspicious days to perform this recitation.
faq.q2: Can women recite the Bajrang Baan?
faq.a2: Yes, women can absolutely recite the Bajrang Baan. There is a common misconception that they cannot, but according to scriptures, anyone with sincere devotion and purity can recite it. Women should follow standard religious hygiene guidelines, such as avoiding recitation during menstruation and maintaining personal cleanliness during the worship period.
faq.q3: What are the key rules for reciting the Bajrang Baan?
faq.a3:
To derive the maximum benefit, practitioners generally follow these rules:
Purity: Bathe and wear clean clothes (red or saffron are often preferred) before starting.
Seating: Sit on a clean mat (ideally Kush grass) facing East or South.
Sankalp (Resolve): If reciting for a specific purpose, take a vow to complete the recitation for a fixed number of days (e.g., 11, 21, or 40 days) without breaks.
Lifestyle: Maintain a Sattvic lifestyle (avoid non-vegetarian food and alcohol) during the period of your resolution.
Clarity: Ensure your pronunciation is clear and steady.
faq.q4: What are the benefits of reciting the Bajrang Baan?
faq.a4:
The Bajrang Baan is primarily recited for protection and empowerment. Its benefits include:
Removal of Negativity: It is believed to ward off ghosts, spirits, and negative energy.
Overcoming Enemies: It helps in neutralising the influence of people intending to cause harm.
Mental Strength: It instills immense courage and confidence in the face of fear or difficult life situations.
Protection: Devotees believe it acts as a shield, protecting them from accidents, diseases, and sudden hardships.
faq.q5: Should it be recited daily or only in specific situations?
faq.a5: Because the Bajrang Baan acts like an "arrow" (Baan) aimed at solving a specific problem, it is best reserved for times of need or specific goals. While you can recite it daily if you live a strictly disciplined and Sattvic life, many spiritual scholars suggest reciting the Hanuman Chalisa for daily devotion, and reserving the Bajrang Baan for challenging circumstances or significant obstacles that require divine intervention.
